listitem.para: Describe the language and/or dialect spoken by native Jamaicans.
Compare and contrast communication styles between men and women of Jamaican Heritage.
Name two folklore that will identify if a woman is pregnant.
What rituals are displayed and adhered to in the post-partum period of pregnancy?
What scale was used to describe varied skin colors?
In terms of biological features, the vast majority of Jamaicans fall into what racial category?
Fill in the blank: Currently Jamaicans are experiencing a ______epidemic fueled by changes in diet and behaviors?
What is the most concerning communicable condition which remains a top 10 cause of death in Jamaica?
Jamaicans perceive the meaning of food in ways that extend beyond its nutritional aspect. Many foods are used to treat health conditions and are therefore viewed as medicinal as well. TRUE OR FALSE
List five foods commonly consumed regularly.
Jamaicans have a strong focus on the use of fruits for healing and for wellness. TRUE OR FALSE
Vitamin D levels are deficient in as many as half of children under 5 years of age and in roughly a fifth of older children. TRUE OR FALSE
Due to cultural beliefs and practices as well as health education limitations, medication adherence is often low among Jamaican patients? TRUE OR FALSE
Statistics show that 73% of all Jamaicans self-medicate with plants and herbs with many being home sourced. TRUE OR FALSE
Jamaicans currently experience how many healthcare systems?
Describe three family values in the Jamaican culture.
Discuss several death rituals and their significance in Jamaican culture.
How is spirituality displayed in the Jamaican culture?
Discuss the use of Patois in the workplace.
Identify social determinants of healthcare in the rural sector.
Discuss the reason for herbal medicine being the first choice for healthcare in the Jamaican culture.
